column2: Copy sheets from the current workbook separately and save individually
Consolidate sheets of this workbook into one sheet
Create sheets according to the current selection
Sort sheets according to the current selection
Rename sheets according to the current selection
Save sheets each into its own file…
Width of the ellipse (diagonal) for the circular arrangement
Uniform shape width (aligned to reference shape)
Uniform shape width (aligned to widest shape)
Uniform shape width (aligned to narrowest shape)
Match width for all linked shapes
Brings all back shapes exactly in front of the frontmost shape
Brings all front shapes exactly behind the backmost shape
The bullet point color is determined automatically based on the text color.
The selected shape is set as the reference shape. Shapes are aligned to the current position of the reference shape.
The first selected shape is placed at the circle center
Reset the file to its last saved state. The file is closed without saving and reopened.
Disables the auto COM release mechanism that is meant to prevent PowerPoint from slowing down during prolonged use.
Disables the popup dialogs of BKT shapes such as Harvey balls, linked shapes, etc.
Defines the position and size of the selected shape as the content area to fit tables into this area. The selected shape is then deleted.
The center of the shapes must lie within the table cell for automatic arrangement.
The center of the shapes must lie within the underlying shapes for automatic arrangement.
The center of the shapes must lie within the text shape and the respective paragraph for automatic arrangement.
The custom area is defined based on the selected shape. This area can then be chosen via the gallery and is kept permanently in the current presentation.
Apply design to the entire presentation
Write the number of results/groups of a regular expression for selected cells into the respective cell.
Change the reference position of selected shapes to the lower-left corner
Adjust the colors of toggle shapes
The selected slide is duplicated for each image file and the file name is set as the title.
The selected shapes each get an internal link ID. The link is preserved when the shapes are copied.
Link the selected shapes for future operations. The link is preserved when the shapes are copied.
The entire presentation is scanned for linked shapes and the first associated shape in the slide deck is set as the reference.
The entire presentation is scanned for linked shapes and the last associated shape in the slide deck is set as the reference.
The selected shapes are detached from the current group without changing the group.
This operation can take some time for large files and many slides!
Copy dimensions of charts, image crops and tables from one object to another
Perform various actions on all linked shapes
Three tabs for the toolbox with all advanced features on a separate page 3
Duplicates the current shape onto all slides after the current slide and links them for future operations.
Duplicates the currently selected Harvey ball.
An optional (conditional) hyphen is a symbol for optional hyphenation. The hyphen appears only at the line end and otherwise stays invisible.
A non-breaking space does not allow a line break.
A narrow non-breaking space does not allow a line break and is used e.g. between letters of abbreviations.
Apply a formula to all selected cells.
Insert a Likert scale as a toggle shape. The fill level and the colors can be changed via the toggle-shape functions.
Transfer a single property to all linked shapes
Select and add a BKT feature folder
Append a text to all selected cells.
Prepend a text to all selected cells.
Truncate a text at the front or back at a given character.
Truncate a text at the front or back at a given position.
Create, distribute and align a tracker from a selection as an image.
Begin a new section per agenda slide.
Apply a regular expression to all selected cells.
Insert options for updatable slide thumbnails

The set spacing is taken into account when aligning shapes left/right.

The spacing is added: when aligning the left/top edge (of the shape to be moved) and when aligning the shape center to the left/top edge of the reference shape.

The spacing is subtracted: when aligning the right/bottom edge (of the shape to be moved) and when aligning the shape center to the right/bottom edge of the reference shape.
Change settings of the selected slide thumbnail
Settings for arranging on tables, paragraphs or shapes
Change settings for the text box
Settings for automatic check for new versions
Settings for the circular alignment of shapes
Setting options when aligning shape tables
Setting options for the edge auto-fixer
Remove non-printable characters in all selected cells (like the Excel function CLEAN).
Remove superfluous spaces at the start or end of all selected cells (like the Excel function TRIM).
Remove superfluous spaces at the start or end of all selected cells using Python's strip function.
Removes agenda slides of the selected agenda; all meta information is deleted.
Removes all agenda slides in the entire presentation; all meta information is deleted.
Removes all agenda slides belonging to the current agenda except the main slide. All meta information is deleted.
